---
title: "Coral Demography Data Cleaning"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(here)
library(janitor)

# Read in observations from 2013-2023 
wide_data_2013_2023 <- read_excel(here("rawdata", "Coral_Deomography_allyears_2013-24_V1.xlsx"), sheet = 1)

# Produce a character vector of the names of files or in 2024 sheets folder 
individual_files <- list.files("rawdata/2024sheets", pattern = "*.xlsx", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to standardize raw 2024 sheets with inconsistent colulmn names/ pull observer from first row above all columns
read_file <- function(f) {
  meta <- read_excel(f, col_names = FALSE, n_max = 1)
  obs <- tail(na.omit(as.character(meta[1, ])), 1)
  read_excel(f, skip = 1, col_names = TRUE, col_types = "text") %>%
    mutate(Observer = obs)
}

# Combine into one dataframe
corals_2024 <- bind_rows(lapply(individual_files, read_file)) %>%
  # Deselect columns with previous year measurements
  select(-c("W", "H", "L", "Status"))  %>%
  # Correct naming convention
  rename(habitat = Hab,
    transect = Tran) %>%
    # Standardize column naming structure across sheets
  mutate(L2024 = coalesce(L2024, L24),
         W2024 = coalesce(W2024, W24),
         H2024 = coalesce(H2024, H24),
           # Standardize dynamics column across sheets
         Dynamics_2024 = coalesce(Dynam, Dynamic, Dyn, Dynamics),
         across(c(X, Y, Z), as.numeric)
  ) %>%
  select(-any_of(c("L24", "W24", "H24",
                   "Dynam", "Dynamic", "Dyn", "Dynamics", "...17",
                   "status", "source_file"))) %>% 
  clean_names()
```

```{r}
wide_data_no_2024 <- wide_data_2013_2023 %>%
  select(!contains("24")) # Remove 2024 columns because they have no data

all_corals <- full_join(wide_data_no_2024, corals_2024, 
                        by = c("site", "habitat", "transect", "taxa", "x", "y", "z")) %>% 
  # Remove any "filler" or empty rows
  filter(!is.na(site)) %>% 
  clean_names()
```

```{r}
# Create new coral numbers for 2024 recruits
new_coral_numbers <- all_corals %>% 
  arrange(coral_number) %>% 
  mutate(coral_number = if_else(is.na(coral_number), 
                                 row_number(), # assign all new recruits a coral number
                                 coral_number))

# Check that each row got a unqiue coral number
length(unique(new_coral_numbers$coral_number))
```

```{r}
# Pivot data to long by years
corals_long <- new_coral_numbers %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(1:8), names_to = c(".value", "year"), names_pattern = "(.*)(\\d{4}$)") %>% 
  filter(!is.na(year))
```

```{r}
# Create dynamics columns
corals_long_with_dyn <- corals_long %>%
  # Separate cells with multiple dynamics into multiple rows
  separate_longer_delim(dynamics_, delim = regex("\\s*,\\s*")) %>% # regex for inconsistencies in spacing
  mutate(present = case_when(
    is.na(dynamics_) ~ 0, # If dynamics column is NA, present = 0
    TRUE ~ 1),
    # Standardizing the dynamics_ labels
    dynamics_ = case_when(
                str_detect(dynamics_, "Fus") ~ "Fusion",
                str_detect(dynamics_, "Fis") ~ "Fission",
                TRUE ~ dynamics_
    ),
    # Make measurement and year columns numeric (force NA's)
    across(c(year, l, w, h), as.numeric),
    
    # Correct corals that were identified as being on the wrong transect
    transect = case_when(coral_number %in% c(1347: 1353) ~ "T01",
                          coral_number %in% c(1361: 1363) ~ "T01", 
                          coral_number %in% c(1366: 1376) ~ "T01", 
                          coral_number %in% c(1466: 1483) ~ "T01",
                          coral_number == 1863 ~ "T04",
                          coral_number == 1872 ~ "T04", 
                          TRUE ~ transect),
    dynamics_ = replace_values(dynamics_,
                               "Dead" ~ "Death")) %>% 
  # Further pivot to make dynamics columns
  pivot_wider(names_from = dynamics_, values_from = present, names_prefix = "dyn_", values_fill = 0) %>% 
  clean_names() %>%
  select(coral_number, year, site, habitat, transect, taxa, x, y, z, l, w, h, obs, note, growth_ratio, dyn_recruitment, dyn_growth, dyn_shrinkage, dyn_death, dyn_immigration, dyn_emigration, dyn_fusion, dyn_fission, dyn_edge)

```

```{r}
# Test how many corals had multiple recruitment
recruit_check <- corals_long_with_dyn %>%
  group_by(coral_number) %>%
  summarize(recruit_occurrences = sum(dyn_recruitment)) %>%
  filter(recruit_occurrences > 1)

nrow(recruit_check)
```


```{r}
recruit_fix <- corals_long_with_dyn %>% 
  arrange(coral_number, year) %>% 
  group_by(coral_number) %>% 
  # Remove any duplicate recruitment for same coral
  mutate(dyn_recruitment = if_else(cumsum(dyn_recruitment) > 1, 0, dyn_recruitment))
```

```{r}
recruit_fix_check <- recruit_fix %>%
  group_by(coral_number) %>%
  summarize(recruit_occurrences = sum(dyn_recruitment)) %>%
  filter(recruit_occurrences > 1)

nrow(recruit_fix_check)
```


```{r}
death_check <- recruit_fix %>%
  group_by(coral_number) %>% 
  summarize(total_death = sum(dyn_death)) %>% 
  filter(total_death > 1)

nrow(death_check)
```

```{r}
multi_death_histories <- recruit_fix %>%
  filter(coral_number %in% death_check$coral_number) %>%
  arrange(coral_number, year) %>%
  select(coral_number, year, l, w, h, dyn_recruitment, dyn_death)
```


```{r}
death_correct <- recruit_fix %>%
  arrange(coral_number, year) %>%
  group_by(coral_number) %>%
  # Correct any corals that "died" but continued to have measurements within 2 years
  mutate(dyn_death = ifelse(dyn_death == 1 & lead(!is.na(l), n = 2) & year < 2022, 0, dyn_death)) %>% 
  mutate(
    # Create binary is measurements are recorded
    has_measurements = !is.na(suppressWarnings(l)),
    # Track whether coral has died at any point before
    died_before = lag(cumsum(dyn_death) >= 1, default = FALSE),
    # New life detected: measurements present AFTER a previous death
    new_life = has_measurements & died_before,
    # Reset the "dead" status when a new life starts
    life_stage = cumsum(new_life) + 1,
    # Create a new coral number for new corals
    new_coral_number = case_when(
      # Any coral that has died but reappeared is denoted by coral number
      life_stage > 1 ~ paste0(coral_number, "_n"),
      TRUE ~ as.character(coral_number)
    )
  ) %>%
  # Group by NEW coral number
  group_by(new_coral_number) %>%
  # Within each life, keep only the first death
  mutate(dyn_death = if_else(cumsum(dyn_death) > 1, 0, dyn_death)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-c(has_measurements, died_before, new_life))

```


```{r}
corals_long_with_coral_id <- death_correct %>% 
  arrange(new_coral_number, year) %>% 
  group_by(new_coral_number) %>% 
  # New variable signifying when a coral has measurements
  mutate(years_measure = ifelse(!is.na(l), year, NA)) %>% 
  # Select the first year of measurements
  mutate(first_year = min(years_measure, na.rm = TRUE), 
         # Create new coral_id based on location, taxa, and first occurance
  coral_id = str_c(site, habitat, transect, taxa, x, y, z, first_year, sep = "_"), 
  transect_id = str_c(site, habitat, transect, sep = "_")) %>%
  select(-c(years_measure, new_coral_number, coral_number, first_year, life_stage))
 
```
